summary: Round-2 fixes resolve all six round-1 actionable findings cleanly. T4-m1 deleteChat failures now log to ErrorLog with chatId+path. T4-m4 commitDraftChat takes filesDir explicitly and validates canonical-path containment under attachments root before any DB work — interface KDoc updated. T4-m5 constructor split is clearly annotated with @VisibleForTesting(PRIVATE) + KDoc explaining the seam. T4-n1/n2/n3 nits all applied. The rename-inside-Room-transaction change is the right call: it deliberately closes the kill-window the spec's two-statement rollback could not. Two deferred items (T4-m2 DAO update queries, T4-m3 suspend getAll) are explicitly tracked in the commit message. The new tests (null-staging, outside-root, message-insert-fails-rolls-back) tighten the AC-A4/A6 coverage materially. No new issues of severity higher than nit.

findings.issue: @VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.PRIVATE) sits on a constructor that is declared `internal`, not private. The annotation is lint-only so behaviour is unaffected, but the lint check will warn callers in the same module that they're touching a 'private' API when in fact `internal` lets them in legally. `otherwise = VisibleForTesting.NONE` (just-for-tests) or `INTERNAL` (matches the actual modifier) would be more precise.
findings.suggestion: Either change the annotation to `@VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.INTERNAL)` to match the declared modifier, or drop the constructor down to `private` and add a separate test-only factory if you want stronger isolation. Today's combination still reads clearly to humans thanks to the class KDoc.

findings.issue: `sweepZombieChats` still uses `chatDao.observeAll().first()` for a one-shot read. The KDoc on this method covers the single-shot/no-PII contracts but does not call out the snapshot-read workaround the way T4-m3 originally flagged. Future maintainers may not realise this is a deliberate stop-gap rather than the preferred pattern.
findings.suggestion: If T4-m3 (suspend `getAll(): List<ChatEntity>` on ChatDao) is genuinely deferred to a follow-up, add a one-line `// TODO(T4-m3): replace with suspend chatDao.getAll() once added` comment next to the `observeAll().first()` call so the deferral is greppable in the source, not only in commit history.

findings.issue: Zombie sweep log line interpolates `chat.modelId` directly. The KDoc above says `modelId` is currently a HuggingFace id (no PII) and asks future maintainers to keep it that way (T4-S4). That contract is enforced only by code-review vigilance — there is no test, no whitelist, nothing that would catch a future migration to user-supplied model strings (custom URL, local file path) leaking into errors.log.
findings.suggestion: Either drop `modelId` from the log entry (id alone is enough to find the row in DB tooling) or add a simple sanitiser (`modelId.takeIf { it.matches(Regex("[A-Za-z0-9_./-]+")) } ?: "<masked>"`). Optional; current state matches the documented contract.

findings.issue: The hand-rolled rollback transactionRunner in `commitDraftChat_renameFailsAfterRoomInsert` snapshots `chatDao.byId` and `messageDao.byChat`, restores on throw. This is faithful enough to catch the regression the test cares about (no orphan chat row after rename failure), but it doesn't model two real Room behaviours: (a) ID counters are not rolled back (Fakes' `nextId` keeps advancing), and (b) the snapshot is shallow — mutable list values shared by reference. Today neither matters because the failing test path doesn't read those, but a future test that relies on rollback fidelity could be misled.
findings.suggestion: Optional: extract the snapshot/restore into a small `withFakeRoomTransaction { … }` helper that does a deep copy and resets `nextId`, so any future rollback test gets correct semantics for free. Not blocking — the current test asserts what it claims to assert.

round_one_resolution.T4-m1: RESOLVED — DefaultChatRepository.kt:164-171 now branches on `!dir.deleteRecursively()` and emits `errorLog.e("history-write", ...)` with both chatId and path. Observable as required by AC-A2.
round_one_resolution.T4-m2: DEFERRED — documented in commit message; out of scope for this round.
round_one_resolution.T4-m3: DEFERRED — documented in commit message; see T4-r2-n2 for a small follow-up to leave a TODO breadcrumb in source.
round_one_resolution.T4-m4: RESOLVED — commitDraftChat now takes `filesDir: File` (option (a) from the suggestion). Containment is enforced via canonical-path startsWith with `File.separator` suffix (correctly rejects `attachments-evil` sibling and `==attachments` self), AFTER existence/dir-type checks, BEFORE any Room work. Interface KDoc on ChatRepository.kt:31-37 spells out the contract. The check correctly canonicalises symlinks too.
round_one_resolution.T4-m5: RESOLVED — `@VisibleForTesting(otherwise = VisibleForTesting.PRIVATE)` annotation on the primary (test-seam) constructor + class-level KDoc explaining the layout makes intent unambiguous. Hilt KSP supports `@Inject` on a Kotlin secondary constructor (it discovers the annotated constructor regardless of position), so no DI regression. See T4-r2-n1 for an `INTERNAL` vs `PRIVATE` precision nit.
round_one_resolution.T4-n1: RESOLVED — three distinct messages now: 'staging dir must live under attachments root', 'staging dir missing', 'staging path is not a directory'.
round_one_resolution.T4-n2: RESOLVED — `private const val ROLE_USER = "user"` at file top, used at line 99.
round_one_resolution.T4-n3: RESOLVED — renamed to `commitDraftChat_missingStagingDir_throwsBeforeRoomInsert` and supplemented with `_stagingOutsideAttachmentsRoot_throws` and `_nullStagingDir_skipsRenameAndSucceeds`.
round_one_resolution.T4-n4: NOT EXPLICITLY ADDRESSED — `attachmentsRoot.mkdirs()` is no longer called unconditionally before rename in the new code path (the rename now happens inside the transaction with the parent expected to exist via the containment-check precondition). Effectively resolved by the refactor — the swallowed-mkdirs concern no longer applies.

new_concerns_evaluated.Hilt KSP + secondary @Inject: OK — Hilt finds the @Inject annotation regardless of constructor position; only one @Inject constructor is permitted and only the secondary has it. Primary being `internal` does not block Hilt, which uses reflection / generated code at the @Inject site. No KSP regression risk.
new_concerns_evaluated.Canonical-path containment edge cases: Correctly handled. (1) Symlinks: `canonicalPath` resolves them on both sides. (2) Trailing slash: `canonicalPath` never returns trailing separator. (3) Sibling-with-shared-prefix (`attachments-evil`): the `+ File.separator` suffix on the prefix string forces a directory boundary, so `/.../attachments-evil/x` does NOT pass startsWith of `/.../attachments/`. (4) Equality (stagingDir == attachmentsRoot): correctly rejected, since equality is not a `startsWith(prefix + sep)` match. (5) Cross-volume staging: `canonicalPath` will be a different absolute path so the check rejects it before rename ever runs.
new_concerns_evaluated.Filesystem I/O inside Room withTransacti: Acceptable, deliberate trade-off. Room's `withTransaction` holds the writer lock for the duration of the block, so `renameTo` runs under that lock. On Android: (a) `renameTo` for same-parent same-volume rename is fast (kernel-level inode update, microseconds); (b) the staging dir lives directly under attachments root, so it IS same-volume by precondition (containment check guarantees it); (c) no other writer can deadlock against this lock because there is no nested Room call inside `rename`. The 'why' is captured in the class KDoc (closes the kill-window). Worth the lock cost.
new_concerns_evaluated.transactionRunner test seam fidelity: Adequate for the assertions made. See T4-r2-n4 for the shallow-copy / nextId-counter caveat to keep in mind for future rollback tests.
